image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Ever wonder why we only see one side of the Moon?
Context visual: It's not hiding. The Moon is tidally locked, spinning in perfect sync with its orbit around Earth.
Evidence visual: This means it takes the same time to rotate once as it does to orbit Earth — about 27.3 days.
Escalation visual: So, while it may seem like the Moon's playing coy, it's just how physics works!
Payoff visual: This tidal locking creates phases, making our Moon's appearance change over time.
Cta visual: Want to learn more cool science facts? Hit that follow button!
